overview: The Marathon County Executive Committee reconvened in open session to receive an update on FEMA disaster declaration status and community assistance programs following the April 2026 storms, and briefly reviewed the County Board Strategic Plan Survey. The committee learned Marathon County was approved for individual assistance but denied for public assistance due to insufficient documented damages, and that 24 Marathon County residents have already applied for FEMA individual assistance.

agenda.item: Return from closed session — nothing to report
6A: Update on FEMA declaration and community assistance work (presented by Sarah, Public Information Officer)
Individual assistance overview: FEMA grants and SBA low-interest disaster loans
SBA Business Recovery Center and FEMA Disaster Recovery Center updates
Public assistance denial — Marathon County did not meet $670,743 damage threshold
Public information and outreach efforts; new recovery resource page at marathoncounty.gov/ia
Supervisor Robinson question on insurance settlements and property demolition deadlines in Town of Ringle
6B: Review and discussion of County Board Strategic Plan Survey
Item 7: Next meeting date — August 13, 2026 at 3:00 p.m.

discussions.item: FEMA Declaration and Community Assistance Update
discussions.body: Sarah, the county's Public Information Officer, presented a detailed update on FEMA and SBA disaster assistance following the April 13–23, 2026 severe storms, tornadoes, and flooding. She reported that as of July 15, 24 Marathon County residents had filed FEMA individual assistance applications, with four filed the previous day. A FEMA Disaster Recovery Center opened July 14 at the Town of Ringle Municipal Center (Monday–Friday, 1–7 p.m.), and an SBA Business Recovery Center opened July 13 at the Marathon County Public Library Wausau headquarters (Monday–Friday, 9 a.m.–4:30 p.m.). A new recovery resource page was also launched at marathoncounty.gov/ia.

Sarah explained that while Marathon County was approved for individual assistance, it was denied for FEMA public assistance — which covers governmental costs such as debris removal, road repairs, and public infrastructure — along with Bayfield, Manitowoc, and Racine counties. The per-capita damage threshold for Marathon County was $670,743.18, but documented damages from the April storms totaled only approximately $271,000. County staff reviewed whether any additional damages had been missed and concluded they had not; therefore, the county informed Wisconsin Emergency Management it would not pursue an appeal. Gibbs emphasized to committee members that the public assistance denial does not affect individuals' ability to apply for individual assistance.

discussions.item: Insurance Settlements and Town of Ringle Property Questions
discussions.body: Supervisor Robinson asked about the status of insurance company settlements for homes in the Town of Ringle that may need to be demolished, raising concerns about deadlines and potential need for the county to extend deadlines or forgive fees. Gibbs responded that he had not received a follow-up from Ringle representatives but pledged to reach out, noting that Sarah had attended a recent Ringle board meeting on these topics. He mentioned that free legal consultation through the State Bar of Wisconsin and resources from the State Commission on Insurance had been made available to affected residents, and that the Town of Ringle was also exploring whether to issue raze orders for disputed properties.

Gibbs presented the item on behalf of Vice Chair Boots, who had to leave to catch a flight. He asked committee members whether anything was missing from the strategic plan survey included in their packets. Supervisor Arseth asked whether the survey would be available online; Gibbs confirmed it would be sent to county board members as a link-based online survey, similar to the work plan ranking process. No content gaps were identified by members present. Gibbs noted a separate public input process would be used.

actionItems: Gibbs to follow up with Town of Ringle representatives regarding insurance settlement status and potential property demolition deadlines or fee forgiveness
Committee members encouraged to direct constituents to FEMA individual assistance (disasterassistance.gov) and clarify that the public assistance denial does not affect individual applications
County board members to complete the Strategic Plan Survey when link is distributed; members with concerns to contact Vice Chair Boots directly
Staff to continue updating the recovery resource page at marathoncounty.gov/ia as new information arrives
Next Executive Committee meeting scheduled for August 13, 2026 at 3:00 p.m.; members asked to submit ideas for future agenda items
title: Marathon County Executive Committee Meeting Pt.2 - 7/15/26
